question: [File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the order parameter being directly interpolated into the SQL string (order by ab.account_id ${order}) by injecting SQL via the order query parameter, bypassing balanceFilterValidator() to extract gossip transaction data from the account_balance table?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the order parameter being directly interpolated into the token subquery (order by token_id ${order}) to inject SQL that extracts additional columns from token_account, revealing token transfer history linked to gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged user submit account.balance=<exact_amount> combined with timestamp=<T> to confirm whether a specific account held a precise balance at a given snapshot, allowing them to verify whether a gossip transaction of a known amount was received by that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged user use account.balance=gte:<X>&account.balance=lte:<X> (exact balance match) combined with pagination to enumerate all accounts that received a specific gossip transaction amount, revealing the set of transaction recipients?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: formatBalancesResult()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the pagination link generation (utils.getPaginationLink()) with the anchorAccountId to enumerate all accounts in order, and by comparing balance snapshots across pages at two different timestamps, reconstruct the full set of accounts affected by a gossip transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user submit timestamp=ne:<ts1>&timestamp=ne:<ts2>&...&timestamp=ne:<tsN> with all known snapshot timestamps except one to force getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to return exactly the one remaining snapshot, confirming a gossip transaction occurred at that specific timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lte operator check (query.includes(utils.opsMap.lte)) which also matches the lt operator string, causing a timestamp=lt:<X> parameter to be misclassified as lte, resulting in an off-by-one error that includes an extra gossip transaction snapshot in the result?
